summary: Do not ship: the R-45 ownership guard is only a stale snapshot, so a concurrent same-repository PR can still have its shared head rewritten.

findings.title: Blocker: exclusive head ownership expires before the force-push
findings.body: `contestedHead` is calculated once from the initial PR scan and reused after sibling reconciliation and commit checks. During that interval, a user or another bot can open a same-repository PR from this deterministic head to another base. Opening the PR does not move the Git ref, so the later `--force-with-lease` still succeeds and rewrites the new PR's shared head and review state—the exact R-45 failure under a real interleaving. W81 only preloads contention before the scan, so it cannot exercise this race.
findings.recommendation: Make rebuild ownership mutation-safe rather than caching it as a planning fact. Prefer immutable, base-OID-addressed replacement heads so no ref is rewritten after PRs can reference it; otherwise introduce a server-enforced ownership protocol, since a Git ref lease cannot guard concurrent PR creation. Add a temporal regression witness where an untrusted different-base PR appears after the first scan and require zero push, create, or arm.

next_steps: Close the PR-occupancy TOCTOU before shipping B-1.
Freeze the temporal ownership witness and regenerate the RED/GREEN records without changing the test between endpoints.
